summary: DeVault argues that the Facebook data scandal should catalyze a broader move away from all centralized commercial platforms, not just Facebook. He contends that the commercial model for internet services is inherently flawed because companies have a fiduciary duty to maximize profits, making user exploitation inevitable. The solution lies in decentralized, federated, open source platforms like Mastodon, where operator incentives align with user interests. He urges readers to systematically disentangle themselves from proprietary platforms one step at a time.
key_points: Facebook is not uniquely evil — Google, Twitter, Snapchat, Uber, and virtually all commercial internet companies spy on users and sell data as industry standard practice
Companies' fiduciary duty to maximize shareholder profits makes every user-friendly promise conditional and ultimately subordinate to profit motives
The combination of decentralization, federation, and open source is the 'golden ticket' to internet freedom, with Mastodon as the leading example
Federated platforms align operator and user incentives because they're run by volunteers with low costs covered by donations, not investors
Switching doesn't have to happen all at once — users should inventory their platform dependencies and migrate incrementally
money_quotes: The solution to the Facebook problem is not jumping ship to another centralized commercial platform. They will be exactly the same.
Nowhere in the equation does it say that they're obligated to do anything for you - the only role you serve is to be a vehicle for exploitation.
There is no promise that a company can make to its users that outweighs the fiduciary duty that obligates them to maximize profits by any means.
Your personal information won't be sold to others, unless we can get away with it.
The operator of each instance is a human being who can be easily reached to give feedback and thanks, rather than a billionaire egomaniac who buys an entire neighborhood so no one can bother him.
It's time to discard proprietary platforms like the manipulative trash they are.
key_insight: The commercial model for internet services is structurally incapable of serving users because fiduciary duty to shareholders will always override user interests, making decentralized, federated, open source platforms the only viable alternative.
